visible_suite_green: n/a — loangen-agent/tests/ does not exist at this base; all tests enter via test_patch, so there is no agent-visible test file to keep green
fairness_audit: Fully hermetic, no mocks: every f2p exercises a public entrypoint (QualificationEngine.qualify / match_lenders, LoanGenPipeline.detect_intent, ContextBuilder.get_missing_data_response) with plain dict/str inputs — no MagicMock, no patched collaborators, no settings touched. _map_qb_financials is referenced by a name that exists at base and is fed a result in the real QBAnalyticsResult shape (total_income / cash_and_bank / top-level dscr), so the tests assert the data contract every correct fix must satisfy, not the oracle's fallback ordering. Coercion tests assert only the sensible observable outcome (missing payment -> 0 debt service -> dscr 20.0; string '2019' -> founded 2019; None year tolerated). Intent tests assert the published cross-module intent identifiers.
litmus_result: Instruction is symptom-framed and publishes only the four intent identifiers (cross-module contract asserted by gold tests) plus the 'numeric-string year is that year' behaviour. It does not name the QuickBooks result fields, the mapping fallback chains, _safe_num, INTENT_DATA_REQUIREMENTS, or the regexes. Difficulty is breadth: a fix for one defect (say, the year coercion) still fails the mapping, missing-data, and 4 intent tests; a literal fix that only handles the one reported crash example fails the string/None permutations.
notes: Multi-defect task mined from the March-2026 commit 869aa67, using the full backend surface (3 source files / 3 subsystems: deterministic qualification, context assembly + QuickBooks mapping, intent routing). The repo image carries full future history, so environment/Dockerfile rebuilds a single-commit git history (rm -rf .git && git init) after resetting to base, hiding the fix commit. 12 f2p across 3 files with permutations (None vs string vs missing year; None vs numeric monthly payment; 4 distinct intents; three mis-mapped QB fields). Distance defect: the QuickBooks mapping reads the wrong keys upstream and surfaces as zeroed financials in the advisor. difficulty=hard.
